hyperedges.label: corvus core runtime units (the agent loop)

nodes.label: corvus (C++ AI agent runtime)
nodes.rationale: In-process, offline-capable, low-latency agent runtime for C++; the moat is local-first + embeddable + MCP-native, not API mimicry. Runs where Python can't (ROS2, game threads, drones, trading loops).

nodes.label: Tool / FunctionTool / makeTool
nodes.rationale: The agent's hands; three uniform sources (built-in, user C++, MCP). Tiny 4-method interface so writing a tool is trivial; execute takes/returns std::string to keep public headers dependency-light. Tools never throw — return 'ERROR: ...'.

nodes.label: Schema builder
nodes.rationale: Fluent builder generating JSON Schema string so tool authors never hand-write JSON. Hand-rolled to keep Phase 0 dependency-free; may switch to nlohmann/json in Phase 1.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry
nodes.rationale: Thread-safe by-name toolbox (std::map + mutex) shared across agents. Kept a dumb map deliberately; access-control policy layered above it in Phase 4.

nodes.label: Memory / InMemoryMemory
nodes.rationale: Conversation history resent each turn because the LLM is stateless. Interface (Strategy pattern) lets storage swap in one line; SqliteMemory in Phase 1. History currently grows unbounded — trimming strategy undecided.

nodes.label: LLMClient
nodes.rationale: Backend abstraction. Native tool-calling shape (response is text OR structured tool calls) plus streaming onToken. anthropic()/openai()/ollama() factories are Phase-1 stubs that throw today so the public API is locked from the start.

nodes.label: Strategy (reasoning)
nodes.rationale: enum { ToolCalling (default), ReAct (fallback), PlanAndExecute (Phase 4) }. enum over string so typos are compile errors. ReAct text-parsing is fragile/dated; native tool-calling is the default.

nodes.label: Agent loop
nodes.rationale: The loop: build tool specs -> append task -> loop{check cancel -> llm.complete() -> if no tool calls done else run each tool, append observations} up to maxIterations. run() blocking + runAsync() future+cancel. Returns RunResult.

nodes.label: AgentBuilder
nodes.rationale: Fluent construction with fail-fast validation; the public face of the API, designed to match the 12-line quickstart. build() throws if no model set; fills sensible defaults.

nodes.label: MockLLM
nodes.rationale: Deterministic fake LLMClient enabling offline, key-free, reproducible tests of the whole agent loop. Serious frameworks ship a way to test agents deterministically.

nodes.label: CancelToken
nodes.rationale: Cooperative cancellation checked at loop-top; required so a running agent can be stopped for ROS2/game targets. Phase 1 threads it into the client so in-flight HTTP aborts sub-second.

nodes.label: ToolGuard (per-tool safety)
nodes.rationale: Per-tool safety primitive inside execute() (path jail / private-IP + scheme allowlist / timeout / output cap). Answers 'is this call itself safe?' — distinct from ToolPolicy. Ships early (P1 HttpRequest); HttpRequest is not a 'safe' tool (SSRF risk).

nodes.label: ToolPolicy / RBAC (access control)
nodes.rationale: Per-agent 'may this agent use this tool?' policy layer above the dumb ToolRegistry; Phase 4 with multi-agent orchestration. Distinct from ToolGuard — don't conflate. Per-agent isolation works today by composition.

nodes.label: MCP-native client
nodes.rationale: Differentiator #1: use thousands of existing MCP servers as tools, solving ecosystem cold-start. Primary third-party extension path (process-isolated, no ABI risk). WebSearch arrives free via a public MCP server (Phase 3).

nodes.label: Native tool-calling + GBNF
nodes.rationale: Differentiator #2: provider tool-use JSON schemas for cloud; grammar-constrained JSON (GBNF) for local llama.cpp. ReAct is a fallback, not the default.

nodes.label: Async + streaming + cancellation
nodes.rationale: Differentiator #3: non-blocking runAsync, token streaming, CancelToken. A blocking loop would stall a ROS2 spin loop or a game frame — disqualifying for the core targets.

nodes.label: Per-tool timeout
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 loop wraps each execute() in a timeout — today one hung tool freezes the whole agent thread, which is disqualifying for ROS2/game/HFT targets. The single highest-value gap to close.

nodes.label: Framework-first roadmap (Phases 0-6)
nodes.rationale: Each phase = spec -> plan -> build. Built-in tools ship by dependency + blast radius: Calculator+guarded HttpRequest (P1), jailed File I/O (P2), guarded Shell (P4). Jarvis assistant demoted to optional post-1.0 showcase.

nodes.label: Design patterns in corvus
nodes.rationale: Strategy (LLMClient/Memory/Strategy), Builder (AgentBuilder), Command (Tool), Registry (ToolRegistry), Observer (AgentCallbacks), Template Method (agent loop). Each solves a concrete problem, not decoration.

nodes.label: Agent loop mental model
nodes.rationale: An LLM is text-in/text-out; an agent puts it in a loop with tools. Build the skeleton first (fake MockLLM brain) so plugging in a real model later is a small safe step.

nodes.label: AgentCallbacks (streaming + observability)
nodes.rationale: Optional hooks onToken/onToolCall/onToolResult/onStep — one Observer mechanism giving both streaming and the cheap version of LangSmith-style tracing.

nodes.label: Local-first embeddable positioning
nodes.rationale: Reframe: framework-first. Original roadmap built the personal assistant first then extracted the library, burying the product. 'LangChain for C++' is the discovery hook; the moat is local-first + embeddable + MCP-native.

nodes.label: Orchestrator (multi-agent)
nodes.rationale: Routes tasks (Chain of Responsibility) + parallel execution via thread pool. Phase 4.

nodes.label: EventBus (agent-to-agent)
nodes.rationale: Observer bus for agents reacting to each other; Phase 4 orchestration.

nodes.label: McpClient (stdio + HTTP/SSE)
nodes.rationale: Discovers a server's tools and adapts each into a Tool, uniform to the agent. Phase 3.

nodes.label: C-ABI plugin correction
nodes.rationale: The original extern C create_tool() passed std::string/std::shared_ptr across .so boundaries — UB across STL versions. Fix: MCP is primary extension path; native in-process plugins, if kept, use a pure C ABI. Leaning MCP-only.

nodes.label: AI Agent Orchestration Framework (original vision)
nodes.rationale: Long-form original design the framework-first spec revises. Covers LLM basics, the ReAct loop, six building blocks, orchestration patterns, LangChain/llama.cpp comparison, and a step-by-step build of the Jarvis assistant.

nodes.label: Six building blocks of orchestration
nodes.rationale: What every orchestration framework needs — Tool, Registry, Memory, Task, EventBus, ReasoningStrategy.

nodes.label: ReAct reasoning+acting loop
nodes.rationale: Reasoning + acting in a loop — the insight the agent loop is built on.

nodes.label: Orchestrator + Chain of Responsibility
nodes.rationale: Coordinating everything; routing tasks to the right agent.

nodes.label: EventBus / Observer Pattern
nodes.rationale: When agents need to react to each other.

nodes.label: The Python wall / LangChain gap
nodes.rationale: Where LangChain cannot go — engine vs framework gap. llama.cpp is the engine that made local inference possible; no native C++ agent framework fills the gap. This is why corvus exists.

nodes.label: llama.cpp integration
nodes.rationale: Full agent stack, zero cloud dependency; the local inference engine behind offline-first.

nodes.label: Plugin system (.so community tools)
nodes.rationale: Community grows the ecosystem via shared .so tools — the seam later corrected to MCP-first / pure C ABI.

nodes.label: Jarvis demo assistant (voice/phone/cloud)
nodes.rationale: CLI -> Whisper.cpp voice -> REST/phone -> ngrok -> Oracle Cloud VM. In the revision demoted to an optional post-1.0 showcase, off the critical path.

nodes.label: corvus CMake build
nodes.rationale: Defines corvus::corvus target from src/*.cpp; CMake+FetchContent = 4-line integration. Options CORVUS_BUILD_TESTS/EXAMPLES/WITH_LLAMACPP. Links Threads.

nodes.label: CI workflow (3 OSes + sanitizers)
nodes.rationale: Build+test on Linux/macOS/Windows across gcc/clang/msvc plus an ASan+UBSan pass. The green badge is instant credibility.

nodes.label: corvus_tests (doctest suite)
nodes.rationale: doctest fetched header-only (SOURCE_SUBDIR trick to skip its pre-3.5 CMake under CMake 4+). Offline, deterministic tests driven by MockLLM.

nodes.label: mock_quickstart example
nodes.rationale: Offline runnable demo using MockLLM — no API key. Real-backend examples arrive in later phases.

nodes.label: Add a tool in 5 minutes
nodes.rationale: Contributor onramp: makeTool needs no subclass; two rules — never throw (return 'ERROR: '), write a good description.

nodes.label: corvus 12-line quickstart
nodes.rationale: Landing-page AgentBuilder quickstart; three tool sources one registry; FetchContent 4-line integration. API-first: design the usage site before internals.

nodes.label: graphify (knowledge graph builder)
nodes.rationale: Turns any folder (code/docs/papers/images/video) into a persistent knowledge graph with community detection and an EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS audit trail. Code via AST (no key); docs/images via LLM. Outputs HTML + JSON + GRAPH_REPORT.md.

nodes.label: graphify extraction subagent spec
nodes.rationale: Verbatim subagent prompt: JSON schema, deterministic node-ID rules ({stem}_{entity}), confidence-score rubric, frontmatter/hyperedge/vision rules. This is the very prompt this extraction chunk was produced from.

nodes.label: graphify query / path / explain
nodes.rationale: BFS/DFS traversal over graph.json with constrained vocab-expansion (no invented tokens), token budget, NetworkX fallback, and a self-improving save-result work-memory loop (LESSONS.md).

nodes.label: graphify incremental update / cluster-only
nodes.rationale: Re-extracts only changed files; code-only changes skip semantic extraction (no LLM); build_merge replaces re-extracted sources and prunes deletions.

nodes.label: graphify commit hook + CLAUDE.md integration
nodes.rationale: Post-commit hook auto-rebuilds the graph from changed code files (AST, no LLM); claude install writes an always-on graphify section into CLAUDE.md.

nodes.label: graphify exports (Neo4j/FalkorDB/MCP/wiki)
nodes.rationale: Optional flag-gated exports: wiki, Neo4j/FalkorDB Cypher (MERGE-safe), SVG, GraphML, stdio MCP server exposing query_graph/get_node/etc., and a token-reduction benchmark.

nodes.label: graphify GitHub clone + cross-repo merge
nodes.rationale: Clone repos into ~/.graphify/repos and merge multiple graph.json into one cross-repo graph; each node carries a repo attribute.

nodes.label: graphify video/audio transcription
nodes.rationale: Whisper transcribes video/audio to text (treated as docs) with a self-composed domain-hint initial prompt derived from god-node labels.

nodes.label: graphify add URL / watch folder
nodes.rationale: add fetches a URL (YouTube/X/arXiv/PDF/image/webpage) into the corpus; watch auto-rebuilds on file changes (code changes need no LLM, docs write a needs_update flag).

nodes.label: brainstorming skill (idea -> design)
nodes.rationale: Hard-gate: no implementation until a design is presented and user-approved. One question at a time, propose 2-3 approaches, write spec to docs/superpowers/specs, self-review, then the only next step is writing-plans.

nodes.label: Visual Companion (browser brainstorm)
nodes.rationale: Just-in-time browser tool for visual questions (mockups/diagrams/layout comparisons); a local server serves the newest HTML fragment and records click selections to state_dir/events. Per-question decision: show vs tell.

nodes.label: Spec document reviewer prompt
nodes.rationale: Subagent template verifying a spec is complete/consistent/ready for planning; flag only issues that would cause real planning problems (completeness, consistency, clarity, scope, YAGNI).

nodes.label: Brainstorm frame template (CSS)
nodes.rationale: Frame/CSS reference the visual-companion server wraps content fragments in — header, theme, options/cards/mockup/split/pros-cons classes and the interactive helper.

nodes.label: claude-mem cross-session memory
nodes.rationale: Injected cross-session memory context block backed by claude-mem MCP search tools; currently empty (no prior sessions).

hyperedges.label: corvus core runtime units (the agent loop)
